taskDescription: Arrange the upper limb bones in order from proximal (closest to trunk) to distal (farthest from trunk).
paragraphs: Clavicle and Scapula — the pectoral girdle bones that connect the upper limb to the axial skeleton.
Humerus — the single long bone of the arm between the shoulder and elbow joints.
Radius and Ulna — the two forearm bones extending from the elbow to the wrist.
Carpal bones — eight small bones arranged in two rows forming the wrist.
Metacarpals — five bones forming the palm of the hand, numbered I to V from thumb to little finger.
Phalanges — fourteen bones forming the digits (two for the thumb, three for each finger).

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
